channel.title: Alex Wilson's writing
channel.description: Alex on engineering, products & everything in-between

item.title: Weeknotes: Iced Honey — Week 11, 2026
item.description: This week I spoke about AI a lot in four entirely separated contexts, and got to run an architecture session I’ve waited a few months for. Pretty good. Our faustian pact is back. And because of it, I had to say a few…
item.dc:creator: alex
item.content:encoded:
• This week I spoke about AI a lot in four entirely separated contexts, and got to run an architecture session I’ve waited a few months for. Pretty good.
  • Our faustian pact is back. And because of it, I had to say a few words during an all-hands, so I said “a few words“.
  • Round tables make for interesting dinner parties.
  • At a nomikai, I spoke in extremely basic Japanese about doing the Yamathon, and some other stuff which I don’t remember, before going to another bar where some animated Australians aggressively and ardently advanced their all-absorbing affable aura... until we left because it’s a school night maaaaan.
  • In a moment of weakness, we went to a restaurant best described as “basically Nandos” and I had their spiciest grilled chicken. In words I never thought I’d use, I actually really miss Nandos?
  • Friday the 13th was Freaky Friday, which like in the movie of the same name, involved some switching of places. Unlike the movie, it also involved unlimited garlic shrimp, two…

Read the full post here...

item.title: Weeknotes: Paranoid — Week 10, 2026
item.description: Like the sequence in every good heist movie, I’m doing the rounds and reassembling the brain trust. What’s the job? Stealing something precious from someone who first stole from me. Hosted a departmental all-hands and…
item.dc:creator: alex
item.content:encoded:
• Like the sequence in every good heist movie, I’m doing the rounds and reassembling the brain trust. What’s the job? Stealing something precious from someone who first stole from me.
  • Hosted a departmental all-hands and the time-keeping was ギリギリ. Since it was last thing on Friday, I closed it with the bit I’ve wanted to do since joining this company: “Have a Good Week-End!”
  • Now the proud owner of a Trader Joe's tote bag. Thanks Danny.
  • One of the appointments made a few weeks ago was kept: We returned for Kabukicho’s best curry and a few rounds of gin. Never break a promise, eh?
  • Clocked off early on Thursday to see a World Baseball Classic match: Korea vs. Czechia. Korea scored a grand slam in the first inning, and maintained a healthy lead the entire game so it was no surprise when they won. But considering that Korea’s team is pro and Czechia’s is not, I thought both sides played incredibly well. And we had a lot of fun at the match!
  • This was my first MLB-style baseball game…

Read the full post here...

item.title: Weeknotes: Breadfan — Week 9, 2026
item.description: Grizzly bears enter into hibernation in late December, emerging in mid-to-late March. I am demonstrably not a grizzly, but I have been hibernating, and now March is here I feel myself waking up. Monday was a public…
item.dc:creator: alex
item.content:encoded:
• Grizzly bears enter into hibernation in late December, emerging in mid-to-late March. I am demonstrably not a grizzly, but I have been hibernating, and now March is here I feel myself waking up.
  • Monday was a public holiday: We went to Yokohama for street-food hopping in Chinatown followed by tinnies in the park at Hammerhead. I’m telling myself it was for LNY, but really I went to be swim in the sunlight.
  • This week I have been repeating the phrases “Academic Rigor”, “Intellectual Curiosity” and perhaps most frequently, “Informed”.
  • My team’s objectives are now all on track.
  • Friday I got some good news, with a buried lede of much better news. And had a few good conversations.
  • Much much later I was given a bottle of clear liquid in a dark alley - Rakia at last! Followed by a martenitsa. Thus marking my fifth year following this tradition.
  • Had a great time in a tiny bar devolving between randomly deep topics whilst saying goodbye to the group from BG.
  • I love Sofia: A place where every…

Read the full post here...

item.title: Weeknotes: Astronomy — Week 8, 2026
item.description: Over the years I have noticed a trend in my blog: During late winter things feel a little wobbly. Alas, the time has come to admit it since the signs are all there: I am seasonally affected. Like a plant I need the sun…
item.dc:creator: alex
item.content:encoded:
• Over the years I have noticed a trend in my blog: During late winter things feel a little wobbly. Alas, the time has come to admit it since the signs are all there: I am seasonally affected. Like a plant I need the sun.
  • My dentist said that I’m “very good at chewing”, which is a brand new category of complement. Thanks, I think?
  • Reader, have you heard of “Specification Driven Development”? I think it’s where you write the specifications for the developer and they build the thing for you, or something like that. Only instead of developers we now call them agents? Anyway, I wrote some specifications this week.
  • Did some more procurement activities this week. It’s fun.
  • Happy Lunar New Year. How did you celebrate?
  • Midweek, one thing led to another and I was made the last-minute honoured guest at another department’s dinner. We made short work of multiple mountains of chicken. The sanjikai was at my new local. My calendar did not survive this experience ~ I now have many new…

Read the full post here...

item.title: Weeknotes: Tuesday’s Gone — Week 7, 2026
item.description: Last week we got a cryptic text message in the falling snow. This week ran the full gamut from bitter cold to early spring. On the docket was database design, architecture review and procurement. Sexy stuff, I know…
item.dc:creator: alex
item.content:encoded:
• Last week we got a cryptic text message in the falling snow. This week ran the full gamut from bitter cold to early spring.
  • On the docket was database design, architecture review and procurement. Sexy stuff, I know.
  • Procurement I hadn’t done in a hot minute. Sales calls are OK, I guess it’s nice to speak to people around the world? What pleasantly surprised me was how much I enjoyed the spreadsheet-heavy cost analysis & forecasting work, which pulled on a skillset I haven’t really exercised since stopping being a manager.
  • And it’s always fascinating/surprising just how many people don’t understand finance.
  • On Tuesday afternoon I received another text message from AG. Less cryptic this time: “MP?” Reader, what would you do? I have never said no to this request, and this was not my time to start.
  • We met at HUB for the customary pint and a jaeger-bomb and ended up in one of my favourite bars in kabukicho for nomihoudai, leaving in the wee hours of Wednesday after 13.7 drinks…

Read the full post here...

item.title: Weeknotes: Trapped Under Ice — Week 6, 2026
item.description: Another week, two trips to two different ward offices. But what you’re really here for: Me being excited about realising the full potential of the C4 model (Context, Containers, Components, Code). Something I first…
item.dc:creator: alex
item.content:encoded:
• Another week, two trips to two different ward offices.
  • But what you’re really here for: Me being excited about realising the full potential of the C4 model (Context, Containers, Components, Code). Something I first encountered in FT.com and have loved to deploy ever since.
  • For a new project, I put everything I uncovered during discovery into a fresh C4 model, before working with the engineering team to develop it into a skeleton. We invited our product manager to the fray and together actualised requirements directly on the model. This was one of the most fun system design experiences I’ve ever had.
  • To greet both the new year and the new joiners to my department, I hosted a new years party (shinnenkai). My first choice restaurant cancelled on me at the 11th hour, but our pivot was alright and we had an absolute blast.
  • My division sent out a newsletter this week. After our first draft was ready, we got into a room. It was mega fun to edit. Words matter, people!
  • Taken to a new…

Read the full post here...

item.title: Weeknotes: It’s Electric — Week 5, 2026
item.description: This week was very ... taxing. I did a lot of thinking, talking and diagramming this week but I know you’re not here for that. This week’s story-arc was a mission to get some paperwork from my tax office. Slight issue…
item.dc:creator: alex
item.content:encoded:
• This week was very ... taxing.
  • I did a lot of thinking, talking and diagramming this week but I know you’re not here for that.
  • This week’s story-arc was a mission to get some paperwork from my tax office. Slight issue? I have no idea where it is, or how my tax works. OK: Let’s fix this. After many hours and after much help, I located my tax office and learned how taxes work. And by the end of the week, after many hours and much reading of old websites and old webapps, I successfully ordered and received my paperwork. Bank transfers to buy PDFs was not on my bingo card for 2026.
  • Thanks to Sake Club I’m starting to get a feel for the dimensions of sake flavours, and know what I like and don’t like. I’d describe my favourite profile as tasting a lot like apple juice.
  • Finally tried Jiro Ramen and it’s an intense affair. Queue, pay, queue again, and eventually be seated. Next up, wait for your number to be called before yelling your order in fast Japanese. Stressful, right? Then you eat quickly, because of the pressure of the people queuing right behind you. Never before did I eat so much garlic so quickly: And after, even with mouthwash, it took hours…

Read the full post here...

item.title: Weeknotes: Moth into Flame — Week 4, 2026
item.description: Reader, how are you celebrating Burns Night? With some help I assembled a passable haggis, neeps & tatties and a cheese board. Some wisdom I was taught a while ago: Whenever you see cheap/discounted sea urchin sushi…
item.dc:creator: alex
item.content:encoded:
• Reader, how are you celebrating Burns Night? With some help I assembled a passable haggis, neeps & tatties and a cheese board.
  • Some wisdom I was taught a while ago: Whenever you see cheap/discounted sea urchin sushi, buy it and eat it immediately. Mid-week, it was sea urchin sushi which fuelled me through a surprise karaoke session, where I piloted The Black Parade.
  • Very nice yakiniku in Ueno. Maybe my favourite memory of Tokyo yakiniku since last summer.
  • You know how the old saying goes about language exchange programmes: Third time’s the charm!
  • Conversation practice genuinely feels like an accelerant and this week as since my class was out, we spent more time in practice, which felt pretty incredible.
  • The Yotsuya Shinjuku crew came back together for one night and one night only. Stalled by three train delays, I joined them late for sake and ratatouille. Even later I got ohisashiburi’d by one of my favourite monks, but didn’t do any calligraphy.
  • Met up in Nakakobu for some Ekiben…

Read the full post here...

item.title: Weeknotes: Eye of the Beholder — Week 3, 2026
item.description: I’m now a wallet phone case guy. Monday was Coming of Age day (a national holiday for those paying attention) and it was coincidentally also the Ome Daruma Market. To add pomp and circumstance to my 2026 resolution…
item.dc:creator: alex
item.content:encoded:
• I’m now a wallet phone case guy.
  • Monday was Coming of Age day (a national holiday for those paying attention) and it was coincidentally also the Ome Daruma Market. To add pomp and circumstance to my 2026 resolution, after some cat-sitting, I ventured all the way to Ome, and in exchange for cash and a few words of broken Japanese, returned triumphant with a Daruma the size of a voluptuous pineapple.
  • Unfortunately I woke up the next day with a throat so sore that eating hurt. This was not a great start to the week, and between working from home and staying (mostly) indoors most of my escapades involved housework.
  • But: One of my chores involved contacting some law firms. Most wrote back. One, with an address in one of Tokyo’s “nicest” business districts, emailed me a quote with so many zeroes that my head is still spinning.
  • Back in Japanese class for this term, and it’s certainly a difficulty notch higher. I can rattle off the self introductions fairly unprompted now.
  • After some wrangling, we re-did the branding for my department for an all-hands presentation. I’m sticking hard to the comms plan I…

Read the full post here...

item.title: Weeknotes: Of Frogs and Men — Week 2, 2026
item.description: Twelfth night burst into my apartment in the dead of the night and bundled my Christmas tree into a small box. Week 2 and I’m both still digesting last year and writing goals for this year. However after a self-inflicted…
item.dc:creator: alex
item.content:encoded:
• Twelfth night burst into my apartment in the dead of the night and bundled my Christmas tree into a small box.
  • Week 2 and I’m both still digesting last year and writing goals for this year. However after a self-inflicted nudge, I have now set my top-priority.
  • For some system design, I learned a lot about how public holidays and time-off legally work in Japan. In short there are three-(ish) separate bodies of legislation which each do a different thing and name different days, but come together to give us weekends and bank holidays. Although, interestingly, unless you’re a bank, none of these days is actually guaranteed.
  • My birthday gift game is getting better.
  • Over a delicious chicken lunch, I'm loaned some house-keys with a fob-attached AirTag. I know that the AirTag is there, obviously, but this is a rare opportunity to see how long it really takes for an phone to notice. How long did it take my phone? 6+ hours. Scary.
  • Ridiculously efficient dental appointment on Friday. In 4…

Read the full post here...
